summary: Excellent soundboard capture of an early‑'69 set with standout Dark Star and solid Morning Dew, though the performance is mixed overall.
review: The gd1969-05-23 recording delivers a clear, well‑separated soundboard mix that many fans describe as “amazing,” making it one of the better psychedelic-era tapes. Musically the show has high points – Dark Star, Morning Dew and a strong St. Stephen – but also some uneven moments such as a loose Hard to Handle and a shaky The Eleven, leading reviewers to rate the performance as average to good.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is judged mixed/average (≈3.5) while recording quality is praised as good‑to‑excellent (≈4.5). Weighted average yields about a 4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers praise great sound, separation and clarity; one notes slight pitch correction needed (–1% to +4%) but overall the board mix is solid. Completeness is a bit lower than earlier archived versions.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Early‑'69 set swings between solid psychedelic moments and a few loose stretches; the second half shines with Dark Star while some mid‑show numbers feel average.
